reviews.review_msg: To be fair it was the first time this class was offered. He was ChatGPT'ing his notes on the fly though and squished the content into the first 9 weeks, which were repetitive and extremely disorganized. He is not used to teaching 2nd year students, and he didn't often go deep into the math (high level only). Example problems were not helpful at all

I don't want to hate on this guy because I can tell he's a respected and accomplished individual and he cares about the success of his students, but he sent me for a rip. Everything AI generated, full of mistakes, misleading, confusing, no available readings aside from typo-full lecture slides. Entirely memorization - no learning of concepts.

He is for the students; he wants you to succeed, but the way the course was delivered was horrendous. Multiple mistakes every class, everything felt very last-minute. This class gave me grey hair. The course needs either a full redo or a different prof. Starting with the hardest material doesn't really work either.

He is a genuine and caring person, however a horrible professor. The class is completely disorganized, his lectures and solutions are just all AI generated. He expects very highly out of students and his expectations are completely unrealistic. Low averages and high fail rates.

Farid comes off as a genuinely caring guy who seems to care about the success of his students, and teaches with passion to drive "appreciation" for the subject. However, that is all the positive that can be said about his teaching as he is very disorganized, blames the students for bad performance, is arrogant about the difficulty of his course.

Farid replaced our diff eq course, claiming high fail rates and low relevance. He teaches topics backwards and just reads AI-generated slides. Tests copy them exactly, so the class is pure memorization, not real understanding, and feels like a wasted opportunity. (this review was just as ai generated as his course)

He is a caring professor, but is too detached from reality when it comes to teaching second-year students. The jump from Calc 3 to MSE 281 was astronomical. On top of a clear spike in difficulty, the teaching style of "starting from the end" does not contribute to success. Labs are a joke and a waste of time compared to MSE250 labs. Good luck :)

Worst professor I have ever had. He told us that course would substitute differential equations, but he started to teach advanced control systems material in the most unorganized way possible. AVOID this guy.
The course is so unorganized. Lecture slides are full of mistakes. Worst professor by a mile.
He is a really Caring and wants you to actually enjoy and learn through your capstone project.

I heard a lot of people complain about him, but honestly he was one the nicest instructors I've ever had. Pays a lot of attention to details but at the end of the day makes sure that you've understood the material properly. If you are willing to put in the work I recommend him 100%
He is a great prof in class, great teaching skills, great knowledge,... but at the end, if he doesnt like you, you wont enjoy the class, nor the markings at all...
This is one of the best instructors you could ever have. Classes are super interesting, very clear, with real like examples. Test correlate with the material covered in class! Just great

This guy is amazing! Our whole class actually stood up and clapped for him on the last class.. his grading is very fair and is willing to help students anytime. Just make sure you understand his assignments well.. each and every question! and you will get at least get a B+.. which is average when he curves..
